circuits.designation: Hall ring final circuit (serves the only general-purpose 13 A sockets in this scope; passes alongside the bathroom but no socket sits inside any §701 zone)
Bathroom shaver supply (BS EN 61558-2-5 isolating transformer) — dedicated radial; 30 mA RCD per cascaded §701.411.3.3 blanket constraint

non_compliance_flags.message: Bathroom (room_type=bathroom, Part 7-701 location) contains a single BS EN 61558-2-5 shaver supply unit on the room's south wall, outside Zone 0/1/2 horizontal extent and at 1400 mm AFFL (clear of Zone 1 vertical envelope). No general-purpose 230 V socket is placed inside the room — design choice keeps the §701.512.3 3 m boundary rule trivially satisfied.

Cascaded from special-locations: 30 mA RCD blanket per BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §701.411.3.3 applies to all low-voltage circuits serving the bathroom. C02 (shaver supply) is on a Type A 30 mA RCBO satisfying the blanket. C01 (hall ring) does not serve any fixture inside the bathroom room polygon but is also on a Type A 30 mA RCBO per the §411.3.3 default for sockets ≤32 A.
All Zs verification deferred to calc.zs_loop_impedance per WI3; both circuits have tool_call_pending_for_zs_verification=true and TOOL-CALL-PENDING:calc.zs_loop_impedance is set in flags[].

assumptions: Ze=0.35Ω and PSCC=6 kA declared by DNO at the consumer cut-out; not yet measured on site.
Bathroom anchor geometry (bath 500-2200 × 0-700 mm, shower head at 2100 mm AFFL) cascaded from special-locations payload; consumer trusts the upstream zone polygons verbatim.
Shaver supply BS EN 61558-2-5 unit is rated IP44 per part-7 best practice for splash zones; the unit's secondary side provides electrical separation per §413.
Hall ring final circuit (~3.0 × 1.5 m floor area) is well within the IET OSG §8.4.4 100 m² recommended limit for a 32 A ring.

Cascade source reuses the lighting-layout shared bathroom payload; engineer-of-record reconciles with a project-specific small-power bathroom cascade when one is authored on the special-locations side (deferred Wave 2 sprint).

drawing_notes: Bathroom (Part 7-701): single BS EN 61558-2-5 shaver supply at 1400 mm AFFL on the south wall, outside Zone 0/1/2 horizontal polygon. No general-purpose 230 V sockets inside the bathroom — design avoids §701.512.3 3 m boundary issues entirely.
Hall ring (C01) 2.5 mm² + 1.5 mm² CPC PVC T+E; 32 A Type B RCBO with Type A 30 mA RCD per BS 7671 §411.3.3.
Shaver supply (C02) 1.5 mm² T+E; 6 A Type B RCBO with Type A 30 mA RCD per cascaded §701.411.3.3 blanket constraint.

rationale.chat_summary: UK domestic bathroom (Part 7-701 location, room_type=bathroom exact-match to fire INV-12) + adjacent hall. Single BS EN 61558-2-5 shaver supply on dedicated C02 radial inside the bathroom, located outside Zone 0/1/2 horizontal polygon. Hall has 2× BS 1363 double 13 A sockets on a C01 ring final. Both circuits Type A 30 mA RCBOs per cascaded §701.411.3.3 blanket. consumed_intents.special_locations_zoning is populated from the upstream PASS cascade payload (5 zones, compliant=true, zero flags). INV-12 PASS — all 4 sub-checks satisfied.

sections.title: Why room_type=bathroom (exact Part-7 enum match)
sections.summary: Sprint D.4's binding condition is that the cascade INV (INV-12) must actually fire on this example. The schema's allOf clause and INV-12's wording both pivot on room_type being one of the Part-7 set: {bathroom, shower_room, swimming_pool_hall, sauna, medical_group_0_area, medical_group_1_ward, medical_group_2_theatre, external_landscape}. The existing uk-3bed-dwelling example uses room_type=bathroom_domestic which is NOT in the set, so INV-12 was N/A there. This example uses the exact bathroom literal so the cascade fires end-to-end.

decisions.label: room_type literal selection
decisions.summary: Set rooms[0].room_type=bathroom (no _domestic suffix). The Part 7-701 enum trigger then activates the schema's allOf clause requiring consumed_intents.special_locations_zoning to be populated.
decisions.rule: Part-7 enum trigger in small-power IR schema allOf clause

sections.title: Cascade prerequisite (special-locations payload)
sections.summary: consumed_intents.special_locations_zoning carries the 5-zone bathroom payload (bath_z0, bath_z1, shower_z1, bath_z2, shower_z2) and 1 rcd_blanket_by_room electrical constraint. The cascade's compliant flag is true, zero non_compliance_flags. Source: electrical/special-locations/examples/cascade-lighting-layout-uk-bathroom/intent-out.json. The payload is engineering-equivalent for either downstream skill — special-locations IR is anchor-driven (bath_1 + shower_1), not fixture-type-driven.

decisions.label: Reuse lighting-layout PASS cascade payload
decisions.summary: No cascade-small-power-uk-bathroom-pass exists at v1.0. The lighting-layout PASS payload is reused since the bath_1 + shower_1 anchor geometry, the §701 zone polygons, and the rcd_blanket_by_room constraint are identical for both downstream skills.

sections.title: Socket placement decisions
sections.summary: Zero 230 V sockets inside any §701 zone. The bathroom contains exactly one BS EN 61558-2-5 shaver supply on the south wall at (x=2400, y=800) — outside the bath/shower zone polygons (which span x=500..2200 and the bath_z2 polygon extends to x=-100..2800 only at y between -600..1300 around the bath, missing the south wall position at y=800 inside the room's 2700×2100 envelope). The shaver supply is at 1400 mm AFFL, which is inside Zone 2's vertical envelope (0..2250 mm) but Zone 2 explicitly permits shaver supplies (it only prohibits socket_230v).

decisions.label: Shaver supply outside socket prohibitions
decisions.summary: BS EN 61558-2-5 shaver supply units are excluded from Zone 2's prohibited_fixture_types list (Zone 2 prohibits only socket_230v). Placement is therefore compliant.

decisions.label: No general-purpose 230 V socket inside the bathroom
decisions.summary: Design choice — keeps the §701.512.3 3 m boundary rule from any Zone 1 boundary trivially satisfied. Two BS 1363 sockets live in the adjacent hall (C01 ring) outside the bathroom room polygon.

sections.title: Circuit topology + RCD posture
sections.summary: C01 hall ring final: 32 A Type B RCBO + 2.5 mm² + 1.5 mm² CPC PVC ring (14 m total run). C02 bathroom shaver radial: 6 A Type B RCBO + 1.5 mm² T+E (4 m). Both Type A 30 mA RCDs per the §701.411.3.3 cascaded blanket constraint — even though C01 does not serve the bathroom, the §411.3.3 default for sockets ≤32 A already mandates 30 mA RCD coverage, so the cascaded constraint is satisfied without imposing any additional design change.

decisions.label: Type A 30 mA on both circuits
decisions.summary: Cascaded special-locations electrical_constraints[0] requires 30 mA RCD for all LV circuits serving the bathroom. The shaver supply circuit (C02) is in scope; the hall ring (C01) is out of scope of §701 but is on 30 mA by the §411.3.3 default rule.

decisions.label: Shaver supply isolating transformer (BS EN 61558-2-5)
decisions.summary: Inherent electrical separation from secondary side per §413 — the shaver supply's primary is on a 6 A 30 mA RCBO and the secondary serves the user-facing socket via the isolating transformer.
decisions.rule: Electrical separation via shaver isolating transformer

sections.title: INV-12 cascade walk (4 sub-checks)
sections.summary: Sub-check 1 PASS (consumed_intents.special_locations_zoning present with payload). Sub-check 2 PASS (payload.compliant=true). Sub-check 3 PASS — walked sockets[] = [bathroom-S01 (shaver supply, type BS_EN_61558_2_5_shaver_supply_unit, not in any zone's prohibited list), hall-S01, hall-S02 (both BS 1363 13 A doubles in the hall room, outside the bathroom polygon, no §701 zone containment). No 230 V socket sits inside bath_zone_1 or bath_zone_2. Sub-check 4 PASS — payload.non_compliance_flags[] is empty so there is nothing to cascade. compliance_summary.non_compliance_flags[] contains only info-severity design notes (not failures), no _cascaded_from attribution required since nothing was cascaded.

decisions.label: Sub-check 3 cross-walk
decisions.summary: 3 sockets total: bathroom-S01 (shaver) clears Zone 2's prohibited list which only blocks socket_230v; hall-S01/S02 are outside the bathroom room polygon and therefore outside every §701 zone in the payload.

sections.title: Honest disclosures
sections.summary: (1) The cascade source is lighting-layout's PASS bathroom payload — anchor geometry is engineering-equivalent for either downstream skill, so reuse is acceptable. A small-power-specific bathroom PASS cascade is deferred to Wave 2. (2) Hall ring topology is on the smaller end of typical UK domestic — the C01 ring has a short run (14 m) and modest diversified load (4.5 A) by design.

invariants.evidence: Ring (C01) cable composition is 2.5 mm² + 1.5 mm² CPC PVC T+E, matching BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 Table 7.1 for a 32 A ring final circuit; 1.5 mm² CPC ratio satisfies the standard ring composition rule.
Every circuit's ocpd.breaking_capacity_ka (6.0) is greater than or equal to parent_db.pfc_at_busbar_ka (6.0) per BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §434.5.1 and IEC 60898-1 conditional short-circuit ratings.

All BS 1363 13 A socket outlets (hall-S01, hall-S02) are on circuits with Type A 30 mA RCD coverage per BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §411.3.3 additional protection rule for socket outlets up to 32 A in domestic premises.

Bathroom (room_type=bathroom, Part 7-701 location) has zero 230 V socket outlets inside any §701 zone. The single shaver supply is BS EN 61558-2-5 compliant. Hall sockets sit outside the bathroom room polygon. §701.512.3 3 m boundary rule trivially satisfied.

C01 (ring) topology=ring with rooms_covered=[hall]; C02 (dedicated_radial) topology=dedicated_radial with rooms_covered=[bathroom]. All sockets cross-reference a circuit_id (C01 or C02) that exists in circuits[].
Both circuits use Type B curve — domestic ring + shaver supply have no significant inrush. No Type C/D justification required.
diversified_max_load_a per circuit (C01: 4.5 A; C02: 2.2 A) is less than ocpd.rating_a (C01: 32 A; C02: 6 A). Diversity applied per IET OSG Appendix A general lighting + sockets profile.
Both circuits have tool_call_pending_for_zs_verification=true (deferred per WI3). flags[] carries TOOL-CALL-PENDING:calc.zs_loop_impedance accordingly.
Every room (bathroom, hall) appears in at least one circuit's rooms_covered[] (bathroom→C02; hall→C01). No orphan rooms; no circuit covers a room without sockets in that room.
drawing_layout.drawing_standard=BS 1192:2007+A2:2016 matches the GB jurisdiction expected default for small-power layout drawings.

No cable-sizing intent consumed in this example (consumed_intents.cable_sizing absent). INV-11 is a no-op per the v1.1 conditional rule; both circuits carry tool_call_pending_for_zs_verification=true to defer Zs to calc.zs_loop_impedance.

Cascade source: electrical/special-locations/examples/cascade-lighting-layout-uk-bathroom/intent-out.json. Sub-check 1 PASS (consumed_intents.special_locations_zoning present). Sub-check 2 PASS (payload.compliant=true, 5 zones, 1 rcd_blanket constraint, 0 flags). Sub-check 3 PASS — walked sockets: bathroom-S01 (BS_EN_61558_2_5_shaver_supply_unit, 1400 mm AFFL) sits in Zone 2 vertical envelope but BS EN 61558-2-5 is NOT in bath_zone_2.prohibited_fixture_types (which lists only socket_230v); hall-S01, hall-S02 are outside the bathroom polygon. Sub-check 4 PASS — payload.non_compliance_flags[]=[]; nothing to cascade so no _cascaded_from attribution required. BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §701.411.3.3 + §701.512.3.

zones._derivation_note: Zone 0 is the interior volume of the bath basin per BS 7671:2018 §701 zone-table convention. SELV-only equipment permitted; no socket/switch/non-SELV fixtures permitted.
Zone 1 is the vertical projection above the bath polygon to 2250mm AFF per §701 zone-table convention. Overlaps shower_1 zone_1 polygon.
Zone 1 from shower_1 head at 2100mm AFF: zone extends vertically from floor to shower-head height per §701 zone-table.
Zone 2 = 600mm horizontal extension of Zone 1 polygon per §701 zone-table convention. Socket outlets prohibited within 3m of Zone 1.
Zone 2 from shower_1: 600mm horizontal extension of Zone 1; vertical ceiling tied to shower head at 2100mm AFF per §701.

electrical_constraints._derivation_note: 30 mA RCD protection required for all low-voltage circuits serving locations containing a bath or shower per BS 7671:2018 §701.411.3.3.
